chatgpt.recommended_mobile_auth_architecture: Use existing server-side OAuth flows with Capacitor external browser and universal links for return handling.
chatgpt.account_linking_rule: Link accounts by matching verified email addresses and prompt users to merge if conflicts arise.
must_implement_now: Add universal link handling for OAuth return in Capacitor app
Extend server-side Google OAuth to support mobile return URLs
Implement Sign in with Apple server-side flow with mobile return
Enhance deep link listener to handle OAuth redirects securely
Add UI prompts for account linking on email conflict
Test fallback to email code login on mobile
Update privacy policy and app store metadata for new auth methods
must_configure_externally: Configure Apple Developer account for Sign in with Apple
Set up Google OAuth credentials with mobile redirect URIs
Register universal links and associated domains for iOS and Android
Update backend OAuth redirect URIs for mobile
Configure app store entitlements and permissions for Apple Sign In
Set up analytics to track login method usage
Prepare support documentation for users on login options
should_avoid_now: Implementing native SDK bridges for Google or Apple login
Using custom URL schemes for OAuth return
Mixing native and server-side OAuth flows prematurely
Adding biometric or device-based login before social login is stable
main_risks: OAuth redirect URI misconfiguration causing login failures
App Store rejection due to improper Apple Sign In implementation
Duplicate user accounts from unlinked social and email logins
Deep link handling vulnerabilities or race conditions
User confusion during account linking prompts
Regression in existing email code login reliability
test_cases: Successful Google login on iOS and Android via universal link
Successful Apple Sign In on iOS with server-side flow
Fallback to email code login when social login fails
Account linking prompt appears when emails match but accounts differ
OAuth redirect handling with app in background and foreground
No regression in existing web login flows
App Store compliance validation for Apple Sign In
implementation_order: Configure Apple and Google OAuth credentials with mobile redirect URIs
Set up universal links and associated domains in mobile projects
Extend backend OAuth flows to support mobile universal link returns
Implement deep link listener enhancements in Capacitor app
Add UI for account linking and conflict resolution
Test Google and Apple login flows on both platforms
Update privacy policy and app store metadata
Deploy incrementally with monitoring for regressions
chatgpt.one_sentence_summary: Leverage existing server-side OAuth with universal links in Capacitor to add Google and Apple login safely while preserving email login and web flows.

grok.recommended_mobile_auth_architecture: Extend existing server-side OAuth flows using system browser launches and https universal link returns via the current AppUrlOpen listener.
grok.account_linking_rule: Link accounts by verified email address returned from providers while preserving the existing user record.
must_implement_now: Enhance AppUrlOpen handler to parse auth callbacks with state validation
Add server endpoint for Sign in with Apple token exchange
Update web auth UI to detect Capacitor and open system browser
Implement backend account merge logic keyed on email
Add mobile-specific error and cancel handling in tRPC auth routes
Register iOS associated domains and Android intent filters
Preserve email code login path unchanged
must_configure_externally: Apple Sign In service ID and key in developer portal
Google OAuth web client with mobile redirect URIs
AASA file for universal links on domain
App Store Connect capability for Sign in with Apple
should_avoid_now: Native Google or Apple SDK bridges
Any rewrite of email code login
main_risks: Duplicate accounts before linking is live
App Store rejection for auth UX
Regression in existing web Google flow
test_cases: Google login on Android returns via universal link
Apple login on iPhone succeeds and links to email user
Email code login remains fully functional on mobile
Web Google OAuth flow unchanged
Auth cancel and error states handled gracefully
Same email from Google and Apple merges to one account
Deep link with invalid state is rejected
implementation_order: Configure Apple and Google credentials externally
Add Apple token exchange endpoint on backend
Strengthen AppUrlOpen listener with state checks
Detect mobile shell in frontend and launch external auth
Implement email-based account linking in user service
Add mobile error paths and fallbacks
Register associated domains and intent filters
Device test all flows before App Store submission
grok.one_sentence_summary: Reuse hardened server OAuth with universal links and email-based linking for minimal-risk addition of Google and Apple login.

gemini.recommended_mobile_auth_architecture: Leverage external browser flows for Google and a native Capacitor plugin for Apple, both integrating with the existing backend, using universal links for return and robust email-based account linking.
gemini.account_linking_rule: Link new social accounts to an existing user if the verified email matches, otherwise create a new user.
must_implement_now: Capacitor plugin for Apple Sign-In (client-side)
Backend endpoints for Apple Sign-In identity token verification
Backend logic to handle Google OAuth mobile redirects
Frontend UI for Google/Apple buttons (web component)
Backend logic for account linking (email matching)
Harden Capacitor appUrlOpen listener for specific auth redirects
must_configure_externally: Apple Developer Portal: App IDs, Services, Keys
Google Cloud Console: OAuth client IDs, authorized redirect URIs
DNS/Web Server: apple-app-site-association and assetlinks.json files
Xcode/Android Studio: Associated Domains, Intent Filters
should_avoid_now: Implementing full native SDKs for Google/Apple without backend integration
Complex multi-factor authentication beyond current scope
main_risks: App Store rejection due to Apple Sign-In implementation details
Race conditions or state issues during redirect handling
Account linking edge cases (e.g., multiple social accounts, same email)
Maintaining consistent session state between web and mobile
Security vulnerabilities if tokens are mishandled
test_cases: New user signs up with Google (mobile)
Existing email user signs in with Google (mobile, links account)
New user signs up with Apple (mobile)
Existing email user signs in with Apple (mobile, links account)
User signs in with Google/Apple on web, then mobile
User cancels social login flow
Deep link handling for various states (logged in, logged out)
implementation_order: Configure Apple/Google developer consoles
Implement Capacitor plugin for Apple Sign-In (native UI)
Backend endpoints for Apple Sign-In verification and Google OAuth mobile redirects
Frontend UI for social login buttons (web component)
Implement account linking logic on backend
Configure Universal Links/App Links (web server, Capacitor, native projects)
Harden deep link handling in Capacitor appUrlOpen listener
Test thoroughly on both platforms
gemini.one_sentence_summary: Implement Google login via external browser and Apple login via native Capacitor plugin, both leveraging the existing backend with universal links for return and robust email-based account linking.
